questions.explanation: The origin is the point where x-axis and y-axis intersect. Its coordinates are (0, 0).
The x-coordinate of a point is called its abscissa. The y-coordinate is called ordinate.

questions.text: What are the signs of coordinates in second quadrant?
questions.correctAnswer: x is negative, y is positive (-, +)
questions.explanation: In the second quadrant, x-coordinate is negative (left of origin) and y-coordinate is positive (above x-axis).

questions.text: The distance of point (3, 4) from x-axis is ________ units.
questions.explanation: The distance of any point from x-axis is equal to its y-coordinate (ordinate). For (3, 4), distance from x-axis = 4 units.
